_note: Official DECA Business Administration Core (Tier 1) performance indicators, 2025-2026 (MBAResearch/DECA Inc.). `id`, `text`, `level` (PQ/CS/SP) and `performance_element` are verbatim from the official list. `definition` is a plain-language gloss (study aid, not official wording): sourced from a community study list where it matched, otherwise authored to fit the instructional area and performance element.

performance_indicators.text: Comply with the spirit and intent of laws and regulations
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire foundational knowledge of business laws and regulations to understand their nature and scope
performance_indicators.definition: Law is a body of rules prescribed by a controlling authority (government) with binding legal force. The law consists of rules that regulate the conduct of individuals, businesses, and other organizations within societies. The law is intended to protect people against unwanted interference from others.

performance_indicators.text: Discuss the nature of law and sources of law in the United States
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire foundational knowledge of business laws and regulations to understand their nature and scope
performance_indicators.definition: Law is the body of enforceable rules a government sets to regulate conduct. In the U.S., law flows from several sources: constitutions, statutes passed by legislatures, regulations from administrative agencies, common law built from court decisions, and executive orders.

performance_indicators.text: Describe the United States' judicial system
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire foundational knowledge of business laws and regulations to understand their nature and scope
performance_indicators.definition: The judicial branch of the federal government interprets, applies, and administers the laws of the US. It is a network of courts at all levels of government. Federal courts include district courts, federal courts of appeals, and the U.S. Supreme Court.

performance_indicators.text: Describe methods used to protect intellectual property
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire foundational knowledge of business laws and regulations to understand their nature and scope
performance_indicators.definition: Intellectual property can be protected through patents, trademarks, and copyrights. A patent: legal ownership of an item or idea Trademark. A word, name, symbol, sound or color that identifies a good or a service Copyrights: legal protection for anything that is authored by an individual or company, such as writings, music and artwork.

performance_indicators.text: Describe legal issues affecting businesses
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire foundational knowledge of business laws and regulations to understand their nature and scope
performance_indicators.definition: Among the legal issues affecting businesses are the following. Consumer protection issues include product safety (for everything from toys to furniture), zoning laws to protect real estate value and quality of life, public health (i.e. at restaurants), and licensing for personal service providers (such as hairstylists and electricians).

performance_indicators.text: Identify the basic torts relating to business enterprises
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand the civil foundations of the legal environment of business to demonstrate knowledge of contracts
performance_indicators.definition: Torts are civil (or public) wrongdoings against a person or organization that causes measurable harm (not necessarily criminal, but can be); usually satisfied by a lawsuit. (fraud, defamation, contractual interference, false advertising, etc.).

performance_indicators.text: Describe the nature of legally binding contracts
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand the civil foundations of the legal environment of business to demonstrate knowledge of contracts
performance_indicators.definition: A contract that is legally binding is considered valid and enforceable in a court of law. A contract is a clear outline of an agreement between at least two parties.

performance_indicators.text: Describe the nature of legal procedure
performance_indicators.performance_element: Explore the regulatory environment of United States' businesses to understand the diversity of regulations
performance_indicators.definition: The rules by which a court hears and determines what happens in civil lawsuit, criminal or administrative proceedings. Describe factors affecting the settlement of legal matters A settlement is a set of terms that the parties agree to fulfill within a certain time frame after conclusion of the trial.

performance_indicators.text: Discuss the nature of debtor-creditor relationships
performance_indicators.performance_element: Explore the regulatory environment of United States' businesses to understand the diversity of regulations
performance_indicators.definition: Debtor-creditor relationships are created either voluntarily or involuntarily. Voluntarily: most of our debtor-creditor relationships arise from voluntary interactions. ... If the payments aren't made, the creditor can often force the sale of the house to satisfy the mortgage debt.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the nature of agency relationships
performance_indicators.performance_element: Explore the regulatory environment of United States' businesses to understand the diversity of regulations
performance_indicators.definition: An agency relationship exists when one party (the agent) is authorized to act on behalf of another (the principal) in dealings with third parties. The principal is generally bound by, and liable for, the agent's authorized actions—common in employment, sales, and contracting.

performance_indicators.text: Discuss the nature of environmental law
performance_indicators.performance_element: Explore the regulatory environment of United States' businesses to understand the diversity of regulations
performance_indicators.definition: Environmental law is the set of regulations governing how businesses affect air, water, land, and natural resources. Agencies such as the EPA enforce standards on pollution, emissions, and waste disposal; noncompliance brings fines and liability.

performance_indicators.text: Discuss the role of administrative law
performance_indicators.performance_element: Explore the regulatory environment of United States' businesses to understand the diversity of regulations
performance_indicators.definition: Administrative Law integrates several areas of law, which include administrative rules, regulations and procedures for government agencies and bodies; the scope of agency authority, in particular individual privacy; and enforcement powers of agencies.

performance_indicators.text: Explain types of business ownership
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply knowledge of business ownership to establish and continue business operations
performance_indicators.definition: There are three types of business ownership: sole proprietorship, partnership, and corporation. A sole proprietor owns and operates a business by oneself. A partnership is owned and operated by two or more people and is jointly responsible for the business' outcomes. A corporation is operated by several people and owned by stockholders.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the nature of import/export law
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire knowledge of commerce laws and regulations to continue business operations
performance_indicators.definition: A tax collected on imports and some exports by the customs authorities of a country. This tax is used to raise state revenue. It is based on the value of goods called ad valorem duty or the weight, dimensions, or other criteria of the item such as its size.

performance_indicators.text: Describe the nature of customs regulations
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire knowledge of commerce laws and regulations to continue business operations
performance_indicators.definition: A tariff or tax imposed on goods when transported across international borders. The purpose of Customs Duty is to protect each country's economy, residents, jobs, environment, etc., by controlling the flow of goods, especially restrictive and prohibited goods, into and out of the country.

performance_indicators.text: Identify sources that provide relevant, valid written material
performance_indicators.performance_element: Read to acquire meaning from written material and to apply the information to a task
performance_indicators.definition: When evaluating material, determine if the content is fact, opinion, or marketing information. Other factors can include validity of research, relevance, timeliness, authority of the author, and evidence offered to support a claim. If you are validating written material, ensure that you have cross-checked information that is provided by that source.

performance_indicators.text: Extract relevant information from written materials
performance_indicators.performance_element: Read to acquire meaning from written material and to apply the information to a task
performance_indicators.definition: Extracting information while reading is a process that consists of three main activities: pre-reading,reading and review. Before you begin to read, determine what you need to know as a result of your reading. Scan the titles, headings, sub-headings, topic sentences and graphics to more efficiently gather and prioritize the information.

performance_indicators.text: Apply written directions to achieve tasks
performance_indicators.performance_element: Read to acquire meaning from written material and to apply the information to a task
performance_indicators.definition: Read all of the written instructions all the way through at least once. Understand the written instructions. If you have a question, reread the instructions or the part you do not understand. Comprehend what the instructions tell you to do. Do not start to follow the instructions until you are completely clear with them.

performance_indicators.text: Analyze company resources to ascertain policies and procedures
performance_indicators.performance_element: Read to acquire meaning from written material and to apply the information to a task
performance_indicators.definition: Locating and reading a company's own materials—handbooks, intranet, manuals, memos—to determine the official policies and procedures that govern how a task should be done before acting.

performance_indicators.text: Explain communication techniques that support and encourage a speaker
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply active listening skills to demonstrate understanding of what is being said
performance_indicators.definition: Verbal and nonverbal cues that show you are engaged and invite the speaker to continue: nodding, eye contact, brief affirmations ('I see', 'go on'), paraphrasing, and not interrupting.

performance_indicators.text: Follow oral directions
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply active listening skills to demonstrate understanding of what is being said
performance_indicators.definition: Listening carefully to spoken instructions, confirming understanding, and carrying out each step in the correct order, asking clarifying questions when needed.

performance_indicators.text: Demonstrate active listening skills
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply active listening skills to demonstrate understanding of what is being said
performance_indicators.definition: There are five key techniques you can use to develop your active listening skills: Pay attention. Show that you're listening. Provide feedback. Defer judgment. Respond appropriately

performance_indicators.text: Explain the nature of effective verbal communications
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply verbal skills to obtain and convey information
performance_indicators.definition: Ongoing communication (before, during, and after customer interaction) Eliminate nonsense like slang or foul language or anything that makes you look less professional Always follow-up; never take anything for granted. "How is that new purchase working out for you?" "Is there anything else I can do?"

performance_indicators.text: Ask relevant questions
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply verbal skills to obtain and convey information
performance_indicators.definition: Posing focused, on-topic questions that fill gaps in understanding, clarify expectations, or gather the specific information needed to complete a task or serve a customer.

performance_indicators.text: Interpret others' nonverbal cues
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply verbal skills to obtain and convey information
performance_indicators.definition: Reading body language, facial expressions, tone, posture, and gestures to understand what a person feels or means beyond their words.

performance_indicators.text: Provide legitimate responses to inquiries
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply verbal skills to obtain and convey information
performance_indicators.definition: Answering questions accurately, honestly, and completely; if you do not know, committing to find the correct answer rather than guessing.

performance_indicators.text: Give verbal directions
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply verbal skills to obtain and convey information
performance_indicators.definition: Communicating clear, sequenced spoken instructions another person can follow, using plain language and checking for understanding.

performance_indicators.text: Employ communication styles appropriate to target audience
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply verbal skills to obtain and convey information
performance_indicators.definition: Communication channels used for business communication include websites, letters, email, phone conversations, videoconferences and face-to-face meetings. Effective communication relies on selecting an appropriate communication channel for your message.

performance_indicators.text: Defend ideas objectively
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply verbal skills to obtain and convey information
performance_indicators.definition: Supporting your position with facts, evidence, and reasoning rather than emotion, while staying open to other viewpoints and avoiding personal attacks.

performance_indicators.text: Handle phone calls in a businesslike manner
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply verbal skills to obtain and convey information
performance_indicators.definition: A businesslike manner on the telephone includes a formal greeting including "Good morning/afternoon", name of business, and name of department or person answering the call. Calls should be answered promptly, take messages as needed, and use the hold button if needed.

performance_indicators.text: Participate in group discussions
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply verbal skills to obtain and convey information
performance_indicators.definition: Contributing relevant ideas, listening to others, building on their points, and helping the group reach its purpose while respecting differing opinions.

performance_indicators.text: Facilitate (lead) group discussions
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply verbal skills to obtain and convey information
performance_indicators.definition: Guiding a group conversation: setting the agenda, drawing out participation, keeping it on topic, managing time, and steering toward a decision or outcome.

performance_indicators.text: Make oral presentations
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply verbal skills to obtain and convey information
performance_indicators.definition: Delivering information to an audience in an organized, clear, and engaging spoken format, using appropriate visuals, pacing, and delivery.

performance_indicators.text: Utilize note-taking strategies
performance_indicators.performance_element: Record information to maintain and present a report of business activity
performance_indicators.definition: Capturing key points from spoken or written sources efficiently—using abbreviations, outlines, or methods like Cornell notes—so the information can be recalled and used later.

performance_indicators.text: Organize information
performance_indicators.performance_element: Record information to maintain and present a report of business activity
performance_indicators.definition: Arranging facts and data into a logical structure (by category, sequence, or importance) so it is easy to understand, retrieve, and act on.

performance_indicators.text: Select and use appropriate graphic aids
performance_indicators.performance_element: Record information to maintain and present a report of business activity
performance_indicators.definition: The goal is to make the data stand out on the page, and to make the data support the main purpose of the document as clearly and strongly as possible. We want the reader to grasp the speaker's ideas and opinions from their words, and then turn to the graphics for support of those ideas and opinions.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the nature of effective written communications
performance_indicators.performance_element: Write internal and external business correspondence to convey and obtain information effectively
performance_indicators.definition: Written communications is the most common form of business communication and therefore requires employees to write frequently and rapidly. "The words we write are very real representations of our companies and ourselves" Janis Fisher Chan.

performance_indicators.text: Select and utilize appropriate formats for professional writing
performance_indicators.performance_element: Write internal and external business correspondence to convey and obtain information effectively
performance_indicators.definition: Choosing the right document type and layout—email, memo, letter, report—for the audience and purpose, and following its conventions.

performance_indicators.text: Edit and revise written work consistent with professional standards
performance_indicators.performance_element: Write internal and external business correspondence to convey and obtain information effectively
performance_indicators.definition: Reviewing writing for clarity, accuracy, grammar, tone, and formatting, then improving it to meet professional expectations before it is sent.

performance_indicators.text: Write professional emails
performance_indicators.performance_element: Write internal and external business correspondence to convey and obtain information effectively
performance_indicators.definition: Composing clear, concise, courteous business email with an informative subject line, appropriate greeting and tone, an organized body, and a clear call to action.

performance_indicators.text: Write business letters
performance_indicators.performance_element: Write internal and external business correspondence to convey and obtain information effectively
performance_indicators.definition: Producing formal correspondence in standard business-letter format (heading, inside address, salutation, body, closing) for external, professional communication.

performance_indicators.text: Write informational messages
performance_indicators.performance_element: Write internal and external business correspondence to convey and obtain information effectively
performance_indicators.definition: Conveying facts or updates clearly and neutrally—announcements, instructions, confirmations—so the reader understands the message without ambiguity.

performance_indicators.text: Write inquiries
performance_indicators.performance_element: Write internal and external business correspondence to convey and obtain information effectively
performance_indicators.definition: Composing a clear written request for information, products, or services that states exactly what is needed and how to respond.

performance_indicators.text: Write persuasive messages
performance_indicators.performance_element: Write internal and external business correspondence to convey and obtain information effectively
performance_indicators.definition: Crafting writing designed to convince the reader to think or act a certain way, using benefits, evidence, and a clear call to action while addressing the reader's needs.

performance_indicators.text: Write executive summaries
performance_indicators.performance_element: Write internal and external business correspondence to convey and obtain information effectively
performance_indicators.definition: Condensing a longer report into a brief overview of its key points, findings, and recommendations so a busy decision-maker can grasp it quickly.

performance_indicators.text: Prepare simple written reports
performance_indicators.performance_element: Write internal and external business correspondence to convey and obtain information effectively
performance_indicators.definition: Organizing findings or activity into a structured document with a clear purpose, supporting detail, and conclusions for a business audience.

performance_indicators.text: Explain how digital communications (e.g., email, text messages, chats) exposes business to risk
performance_indicators.performance_element: Write internal and external business correspondence to convey and obtain information effectively
performance_indicators.definition: Leave humor out of emails to avoid misinterpretation. External tools risk exposing private, confidential, trademarked, financial, or other sensitive information to the public or competitors. Another negative is the use of the platforms for personal business.

performance_indicators.text: Adapt written correspondence to targeted audiences
performance_indicators.performance_element: Write internal and external business correspondence to convey and obtain information effectively
performance_indicators.definition: Written correspondence should be written similarly to an oral presentation when directing it to a certain audience through methods such as humor and strong emotion. Writing for an audience is usually as simple as maintaining awareness of them while you write. For example, observe age, location, income level, etc.

performance_indicators.text: Use data visualization techniques (e.g., infographics, heat-maps, dynamic model outputs)
performance_indicators.performance_element: Write internal and external business correspondence to convey and obtain information effectively
performance_indicators.definition: Presenting data graphically—charts, infographics, heat-maps, dashboards—so patterns and insights are easy to grasp and support the message.

performance_indicators.text: Describe the impact of a person's social media brand on the achievement of organizational objectives
performance_indicators.performance_element: Use social media to communicate with a business's stakeholders
performance_indicators.definition: An individual's online presence and reputation can help or harm their employer; professional, consistent social media supports company goals, while careless posts can damage trust and image.

performance_indicators.text: Distinguish between using social media for business and personal purposes
performance_indicators.performance_element: Use social media to communicate with a business's stakeholders
performance_indicators.definition: Social media can be used personally to communicate with friends and family, while professionally to promote products and events. - businesses use social media to advertise their company and to get connected with their customers/users and personal purposes are like email, and for employees to use for their daily life.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the nature of staff communication
performance_indicators.performance_element: Communicate with staff to clarify workplace objectives
performance_indicators.definition: Allows employees to receive regular updates and remain current with business issues Allows employee conflicts and issues to be resolved Keeps all employees "on the same page" in regards to company policies, procedures, promotional themes, etc.

performance_indicators.text: Choose and use appropriate channel for workplace communication
performance_indicators.performance_element: Communicate with staff to clarify workplace objectives
performance_indicators.definition: To determine the appropriate communication channel, identify the people you want to communicate with, research how they obtain information, consider the complexity of the message you want to communicate, calculate the cost of communicating and decide whether you want the communication to be interactive.

performance_indicators.text: Participate in a staff meeting
performance_indicators.performance_element: Communicate with staff to clarify workplace objectives
performance_indicators.definition: Engaging productively in a workplace meeting—coming prepared, contributing relevant input, listening, and following up on assigned actions.

performance_indicators.text: Participate in problem-solving groups
performance_indicators.performance_element: Communicate with staff to clarify workplace objectives
performance_indicators.definition: Collaborating with coworkers to define a problem, generate and evaluate options, and agree on a solution.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the nature of positive customer relations
performance_indicators.performance_element: Foster positive relationships with customers to enhance company image
performance_indicators.definition: Customers are the life of any business; any interaction should be positive, courteous, and respectful. Positive customer relations can include showing appreciation, delivering timely service, training employees effectively to interact with clients, and appeasing any concerns brought forward.

performance_indicators.text: Demonstrate a customer service mindset
performance_indicators.performance_element: Foster positive relationships with customers to enhance company image
performance_indicators.definition: Customer is always right! (Keep that positive attitude.) Customers are a business's most important concern; without them there is no business and no jobs. Customer service is an investment, not a cost.

performance_indicators.text: Develop rapport with customers
performance_indicators.performance_element: Foster positive relationships with customers to enhance company image
performance_indicators.definition: Building a friendly, trusting connection with customers through warmth, attentiveness, personalization, and genuine interest, which encourages loyalty and repeat business.

performance_indicators.text: Reinforce service orientation through communication
performance_indicators.performance_element: Foster positive relationships with customers to enhance company image
performance_indicators.definition: Customer surveys are used to pinpoint areas for improvement Employee surveys are also good to pinpoint areas for improvement or of concern Follow-up procedures with customers can help strengthen loyalty

performance_indicators.text: Respond to customer inquiries
performance_indicators.performance_element: Foster positive relationships with customers to enhance company image
performance_indicators.definition: Acknowledge what the customer has said (clarify). Demonstrate your willingness to help the customer. Reply promptly and completely. Don't know? Assure them you'll get the answer. Go the extra mile for them. "I know you didn't ask for this, but I thought you might like . . . "

performance_indicators.text: Adapt communication to the cultural and social differences among clients
performance_indicators.performance_element: Foster positive relationships with customers to enhance company image
performance_indicators.definition: Begin by respecting differences among people. Another key is to avoid and overcome stereotypes. A stereotype is an oversimplified, distorted belief about a person or group. Keep an open mind and treat people with respect and you will maintain smooth relationships.

performance_indicators.text: Interpret business policies to customers/clients
performance_indicators.performance_element: Foster positive relationships with customers to enhance company image
performance_indicators.definition: Explain company procedures to customers, especially to those who are already irritated and/or upset, is difficult. Acknowledge the customers' frustration and use clear, direct & simple language to describe what's required from the customer to proceed with their issue.

performance_indicators.text: Build and maintain relationships with customers
performance_indicators.performance_element: Foster positive relationships with customers to enhance company image
performance_indicators.definition: Focus on Exceptional Communication (timely and efficient) Always follow through and do what you promise Maintain a positive attitude Share knowledge Be open Exceed Expectations Describe factors used by marketers to position products/services The focus of product/service positioning is to set the product apart from the competition.

performance_indicators.text: Handle difficult customers
performance_indicators.performance_element: Resolve conflicts with/for customers to encourage repeat business
performance_indicators.definition: Most important - listen; build a rapport through empathy (put yourself in the customer's shoes); lower your voice, assume all your customers are watching; know when to give in; never get angry or upset; never take it personally; remember that you're interacting with a human; if you promise a callback - call back; summarize the next steps, to be clear.

performance_indicators.text: Handle customer/client complaints
performance_indicators.performance_element: Resolve conflicts with/for customers to encourage repeat business
performance_indicators.definition: Listen & understand; empathize with their situation; offer a solution; execute the solution; and follow-up. There are a variety of difficult customers including those who are angry, impatient, demanding, and indecisive.

performance_indicators.text: Identify company's brand promise
performance_indicators.performance_element: Reinforce company's image to exhibit the company's brand promise
performance_indicators.definition: Brand promise: benefits and experiences that marketing campaigns try to associate with a product in its current and prospective consumers' minds (acts as an extension of the company's positioning). A brand promise is a value or experience a company's customers can expect to receive every single time they interact with that company.

performance_indicators.text: Determine ways of reinforcing the company's image through employee performance
performance_indicators.performance_element: Reinforce company's image to exhibit the company's brand promise
performance_indicators.definition: Employee performance can reinforce a company's image through suggestion selling, order processing and fulfillment, and effective selling skills. Suggestion selling is useful for making the customer happier with his/her original purchase. Sales employees can improve customer satisfaction by the speed and care in which they handle orders.

performance_indicators.text: Discuss the nature of customer relationship management
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand the nature of customer relationship management to show its contributions to a company
performance_indicators.definition: The goal of Customer Relationship Management (CRM) is to maximize the value and effectiveness of all opportunities that businesses have to connect with customers, provide outstanding service to customers, maximize customer satisfaction, attract and retain loyal customers, cultivate and maintain positive, profitable relationships with customers, and develop individualized relationships with customers.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the role of ethics in customer relationship management
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand the nature of customer relationship management to show its contributions to a company
performance_indicators.definition: Ethical CRM means handling customer data honestly and securely, being truthful in communications, respecting privacy, and treating customers fairly to build lasting trust.

performance_indicators.text: Describe the use of technology in customer relationship management
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand the nature of customer relationship management to show its contributions to a company
performance_indicators.definition: Technology serves as the partner of CRM in order to cater the needs of the customers. It makes their relationship meaningful in a way that the company understands what the customers need. It gathers information from the customers through the use of the internet that makes communication faster.

performance_indicators.text: Distinguish between economic goods and services
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand fundamental economic concepts to obtain a foundation for employment in business
performance_indicators.definition: A good is something that you can use or consume. You buy a good with the idea that you will use it, either just once or over and over again. A service is something that someone does for you, like give you a haircut or fix you dinner. Another way to identify the difference is that a good is tangible and a service is

performance_indicators.text: Explain the concept of economic resources
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand fundamental economic concepts to obtain a foundation for employment in business
performance_indicators.definition: Resources used in the production of goods and services, which include: Land or Natural Resources: naturally-occurring goods such as soil and minerals Labor: human effort, which also includes technical and marketing expertise Capital Goods: goods used in the production of other goods Explain concept of risk Risk is related to the expected losses which can be caused by a risky event and to the probability of this event occurring.

performance_indicators.text: Describe the concepts of economics and economic activities
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand fundamental economic concepts to obtain a foundation for employment in business
performance_indicators.definition: Economics deals with the production, distribution, and consumption of goods and services. Economic activities include": manufacturing, buying, selling, transportation, and investing.

performance_indicators.text: Determine economic utilities created by business activities
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand fundamental economic concepts to obtain a foundation for employment in business
performance_indicators.definition: The ability of a good or service to satisfy one or more needs or wants of a consumer (ex: Form - product/service provides use or value; Place - availability is local or convenient; Time - customers can get when they need; Possession - easy for customers to pay for/own product.)

performance_indicators.text: Explain the principles of supply and demand
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand fundamental economic concepts to obtain a foundation for employment in business
performance_indicators.definition: In a market-oriented economy like in the United States, supply and demand determine the prices and quantities of goods and services produced. Supply is the amount of goods and services producers are willing to make and sell. Demand is the willingness and ability of consumers to buy goods and services.

performance_indicators.text: Describe the functions of prices in markets
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand fundamental economic concepts to obtain a foundation for employment in business
performance_indicators.definition: Prices signal value, ration scarce goods, and motivate producers. They communicate information to buyers and sellers, allocate resources, and provide incentives that balance supply and demand.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the role of business in society
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand the nature of business to show its contributions to society
performance_indicators.definition: The role of business in society is "to innovate and deliver products and services, to use resources efficiently so that the value is created, and to conduct operations so they are performed profitability and accepted by society." In summary, "the role of business in society is a coherent way of thinking about a company's impact and interaction with society." Niall Fitzgerald KBE and Mandy Cormack, The Role of Business in Society: An Agenda for Action; Harvard John F.

performance_indicators.text: Describe types of business activities
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand the nature of business to show its contributions to society
performance_indicators.definition: There are six general business activities. Generating ideas is an important activity in that businesses must remain competitive with other firms. Businesses also need to raise capital to finance their operations. Another important business activity is employing and training human resources. Businesses buy and sell goods and services.

performance_indicators.text: Describe types of business models
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand the nature of business to show its contributions to society
performance_indicators.definition: Subscription model customer pays a recurring payment on a monthly basis for access to a service or product. Franchise model A franchise is an established business blueprint that is simply purchased and reproduced by the buyer, the franchisee.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the organizational design of businesses
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand the nature of business to show its contributions to society
performance_indicators.definition: How a company structures roles, authority, and departments—functional, divisional, or matrix—to coordinate work and meet objectives.

performance_indicators.text: Discuss the global environment in which businesses operate
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand the nature of business to show its contributions to society
performance_indicators.definition: Businesses operate amid international competition, trade, cultures, currencies, and regulations; understanding this global context shapes strategy and opportunity.

performance_indicators.text: Describe factors that affect the business environment
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand the nature of business to show its contributions to society
performance_indicators.definition: External forces—economic, social, technological, political/legal, and competitive—that influence how a business operates and the decisions it must make.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the nature of business ethics
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand the nature of business to show its contributions to society
performance_indicators.definition: Business ethics is important to making the general public happy about what happens in the business world (public relations). It creates a good reputation, balances the needs of the stakeholders with the needs of the company, helps the business thrive in the global economy, helps to retain employees, and keeps the business from being penalized under the law.

performance_indicators.text: Explain how organizations adapt to today's markets
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand the nature of business to show its contributions to society
performance_indicators.definition: Organizations adapt to today's markets by using tools like SWOT Analysis and environmental scans to identify ways the business can improve. A SWOT Analysis assesses a company's strengths and weaknesses and the opportunities and threats that surround it.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the types of economic systems
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand economic systems to be able to recognize the environments in which businesses function
performance_indicators.definition: There are four types of economic systems commonly studied. A traditional economy relies on custom and ritual to make its choices. A market economy relies on the consumption choice of consumers. A command economy relies on the government making all the choices for consumers.

performance_indicators.text: Identify the impact of small business/entrepreneurship on market economies
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand economic systems to be able to recognize the environments in which businesses function
performance_indicators.definition: Small businesses diversify market economies by claiming small parts of market share which may appeal to a more specific demographic and consumers.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the concept of private enterprise
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand economic systems to be able to recognize the environments in which businesses function
performance_indicators.definition: A private-enterprise (free-market) system lets individuals own businesses and property, make their own economic choices, compete, and earn profit with limited government control.

performance_indicators.text: Identify factors affecting a business's profit
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand economic systems to be able to recognize the environments in which businesses function
performance_indicators.definition: Sales and expenses There are two ways to increase profit: Increase revenue Decrease expenses

performance_indicators.text: Determine factors affecting business risk
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand economic systems to be able to recognize the environments in which businesses function
performance_indicators.definition: Risk is the possibility of incurring a loss. Economic risks are those that can result in a financial loss. Pure risk is one that presents the chance of loss but no opportunity for gain. Speculative risks offer the chance to either gain or lose. Some risks are controllable, while others are not.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the concept of competition
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand economic systems to be able to recognize the environments in which businesses function
performance_indicators.definition: Seen as the pillar of capitalism in that it may stimulate innovation, encourage efficiency, or drive down prices Competition causes firms to develop new products, services, and technologies This gives consumers greater selection and better products The greater selection typically causes lower prices for the products compared to what the price would be if there were no

performance_indicators.text: Determine the relationship between government and business
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire knowledge of the impact of government on business activities to make informed economic decisions
performance_indicators.definition: The government serves as both a protector and a regulator of business in a free market economy. It serves to protect business property, enforce contracts and settle disagreements through the courts, and collect taxes on the products businesses sell.

performance_indicators.text: Describe the nature of taxes
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire knowledge of the impact of government on business activities to make informed economic decisions
performance_indicators.definition: Taxes are compulsory payments to government used to fund public goods and services. Types include income, sales, property, and payroll taxes, each affecting businesses and consumers differently.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the concept of productivity
performance_indicators.performance_element: Analyze cost/profit relationships to guide business decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: Productivity is the output of work, per worker, often measured per hour Most obvious method to increase productivity involves automation and computerization, which minimize the tasks performed by employees Other methods of improving productivity are advanced employee training or new, easier-to-use equipment Training enables employees to work quicker and smarter Explain nature

performance_indicators.text: Analyze impact of specialization/division of labor on productivity
performance_indicators.performance_element: Analyze cost/profit relationships to guide business decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: Dividing work into specialized tasks lets workers become faster and more skilled, raising output and efficiency—though overspecialization can reduce flexibility and morale.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the concept of organized labor and business
performance_indicators.performance_element: Analyze cost/profit relationships to guide business decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: Organized labor refers to workers uniting in unions to bargain collectively with employers over wages, hours, and conditions, balancing power between labor and business.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the impact of the law of diminishing returns
performance_indicators.performance_element: Analyze cost/profit relationships to guide business decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: Beyond a point, adding more of one input (such as labor) to fixed resources yields smaller and smaller increases in output, raising per-unit cost and guiding how much to produce.

performance_indicators.text: Discuss the measure of consumer spending as an economic indicator
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand economic indicators to recognize economic trends and conditions
performance_indicators.definition: Consumer spending—how much households buy—drives most economic activity; rising spending signals a growing economy, while falling spending can signal a slowdown.

performance_indicators.text: Describe the economic impact of inflation on business
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand economic indicators to recognize economic trends and conditions
performance_indicators.definition: Inflation, a general rise in prices, raises input and labor costs, erodes purchasing power, complicates pricing and planning, and can shrink real profits.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the concept of Gross Domestic Product (GDP)
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand economic indicators to recognize economic trends and conditions
performance_indicators.definition: GDP is the total market value of all goods and services a nation produces in a period. It is the primary measure of economic size and growth.

performance_indicators.text: Discuss the impact of a nation's unemployment rates
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand economic indicators to recognize economic trends and conditions
performance_indicators.definition: The unemployment rate—the share of job-seekers without work—reflects economic health; high unemployment lowers spending and confidence, while very low rates can pressure wages.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the economic impact of interest-rate fluctuations
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand economic indicators to recognize economic trends and conditions
performance_indicators.definition: Interest rates set the cost of borrowing; lower rates encourage spending and investment, higher rates slow them—affecting business expansion, loans, and consumer demand.

performance_indicators.text: Determine the impact of business cycles on business activities
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand economic indicators to recognize economic trends and conditions
performance_indicators.definition: Economies move through expansion, peak, contraction, and trough. Where the economy sits in this cycle affects sales, hiring, investment, and risk.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the nature of global trade
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand global trade's impact to aid business decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: Any trade conducted between different self-governing states. One nation will purchase goods and materials it doesn't produce and sell goods or materials it produces to other nations. The effects of global trade on retailing Global trade means an expansion of its customer base for retailers.

performance_indicators.text: Discuss the impact of globalization on business
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand global trade's impact to aid business decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: Globalization—the growing integration of markets across borders—expands customers and suppliers but increases competition, complexity, and exposure to global events.

performance_indicators.text: Describe the determinants of exchange rates and their effects on the domestic economy
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand global trade's impact to aid business decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: Exchange rates—shaped by trade balances, interest rates, inflation, and demand for a currency—affect import/export prices, competitiveness, and inflation at home.

performance_indicators.text: Explain cultural considerations that impact global business relations
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand global trade's impact to aid business decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: Differences in language, customs, etiquette, values, and negotiation styles shape international business; respecting them builds trust and avoids costly missteps.

performance_indicators.text: Discuss the impact of cultural and social environments on global trade
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand global trade's impact to aid business decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: A society's customs, beliefs, tastes, and norms influence what products sell, how they are marketed, and how business is conducted across countries.

performance_indicators.text: Describe the impact of electronic communication tools (e.g., internet, video- and computer- conferencing, webcasts, email) on global business activities
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand global trade's impact to aid business decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: Internet, email, video- and computer-conferencing, and webcasts let businesses coordinate, market, and trade worldwide instantly, lowering the barriers of distance and time.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the impact of major trade alliances on business activities
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand global trade's impact to aid business decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: Trade alliances and agreements (e.g., USMCA, the EU) reduce tariffs and barriers among members, opening markets and shaping where businesses source and sell.

performance_indicators.text: Describe the impact of the political environment on world trade
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand global trade's impact to aid business decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: Government stability, policies, tariffs, sanctions, and relations between nations can open or restrict international markets and create risk for businesses.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the impact of geography on world trade
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand global trade's impact to aid business decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: Location, climate, natural resources, and access to ports and transport routes shape what nations produce, trade, and how goods move globally.

performance_indicators.text: Describe the impact of a country's history on world trade
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand global trade's impact to aid business decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: A nation's past—colonial ties, conflicts, alliances, and traditions—shapes its trading partners, industries, and attitudes toward foreign business.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the impact of a country's economic development on world trade
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand global trade's impact to aid business decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: A country's level of development affects what it produces and buys—developed nations trade advanced goods and services, while developing nations often export raw materials.

performance_indicators.text: Discuss the impact of bribery and foreign monetary payments on business
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand global trade's impact to aid business decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: Bribery distorts fair competition, is illegal under laws like the Foreign Corrupt Practices Act, and exposes businesses to legal penalties and reputational damage in global dealings.

performance_indicators.text: Identify requirements for international business travel (e.g., passport, visa, proof of citizenship, immunizations, and sponsorship letters)
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand global trade's impact to aid business decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: International travel may require a valid passport, visas, proof of citizenship, immunizations, and sponsorship letters; requirements vary by destination and purpose.

performance_indicators.text: Describe the nature of emotional intelligence
performance_indicators.performance_element: Foster self-understanding to recognize the impact of personal feelings on others
performance_indicators.definition: Emotional intelligence is the ability to recognize, understand, and manage your own emotions and to perceive and respond effectively to others' emotions—key to strong workplace relationships.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the concept of self-esteem
performance_indicators.performance_element: Foster self-understanding to recognize the impact of personal feelings on others
performance_indicators.definition: Self-esteem is the confidence in one's own worth or abilities; self-respect. Compliment employees on a job well done; give them some recognition in front of peers. Pumping up someone's self-esteem helps create a more enthusiastic and loyal employee.

performance_indicators.text: Recognize and overcome personal biases and stereotypes
performance_indicators.performance_element: Foster self-understanding to recognize the impact of personal feelings on others
performance_indicators.definition: Identifying your own unfair assumptions about people or groups and consciously setting them aside to judge individuals fairly and treat everyone with respect.

performance_indicators.text: Assess personal strengths and weaknesses
performance_indicators.performance_element: Foster self-understanding to recognize the impact of personal feelings on others
performance_indicators.definition: Honestly evaluating your own skills, traits, and limitations so you can build on what you do well and improve the areas that hold you back.

performance_indicators.text: Assess personal behavior and values
performance_indicators.performance_element: Foster self-understanding to recognize the impact of personal feelings on others
performance_indicators.definition: Reflecting on how you act and what principles guide you, so your conduct aligns with your values and with professional expectations.

performance_indicators.text: Identify desirable personality traits important to business
performance_indicators.performance_element: Develop personal traits to foster career advancement
performance_indicators.definition: Be persistent, learn to easily adapt to change, assess risks, continue to learn, welcome responsibility, be creative, take charge, know your numbers, don't procrastinate, become self-reliant, and focus on the business not your personal needs.

performance_indicators.text: Exhibit self-confidence
performance_indicators.performance_element: Develop personal traits to foster career advancement
performance_indicators.definition: Self-confidence can be demonstrated by how you present yourself and through your actions. It is shown in your personal hygiene and the way you dress. It can also be shown through your relationships with others and your work habits.

performance_indicators.text: Demonstrate interest and enthusiasm
performance_indicators.performance_element: Develop personal traits to foster career advancement
performance_indicators.definition: Enthusiasm for one's job is interest on steroids. Positive outlook, "I can" attitude, cooperative with others, willingness to listen & learn & try new things. Showing enthusiasm is smiling, eye contact, upbeat manner, etc. Showing interest is much more subdued; you are curious or you show that you like or approve something. That's it.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the importance of demonstrating initiative
performance_indicators.performance_element: Develop personal traits to foster career advancement
performance_indicators.definition: Initiative—taking action and seeking responsibility without being told—shows motivation and reliability, drives results, and stands out to employers.

performance_indicators.text: Demonstrate honesty and integrity
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply ethics to demonstrate trustworthiness
performance_indicators.definition: honesty is about a fair adherence to the facts integrity is about righteous conduct

performance_indicators.text: Demonstrate responsible behavior
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply ethics to demonstrate trustworthiness
performance_indicators.definition: Being dependable and accountable: meeting obligations, following through on commitments, and accepting the consequences of your actions.

performance_indicators.text: Demonstrate fairness
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply ethics to demonstrate trustworthiness
performance_indicators.definition: Treating people impartially and consistently, giving equal consideration and opportunity, and making decisions free of favoritism or bias.

performance_indicators.text: Assess risks of personal decisions
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply ethics to demonstrate trustworthiness
performance_indicators.definition: Weighing the possible costs, benefits, and consequences of a choice before acting, so decisions are thoughtful rather than impulsive.

performance_indicators.text: Demonstrate ethical work habits
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply ethics to demonstrate trustworthiness
performance_indicators.definition: Consistently acting with honesty, integrity, punctuality, and respect on the job—doing the right thing even when no one is watching.

performance_indicators.text: Take responsibility for decisions and actions
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply ethics to demonstrate trustworthiness
performance_indicators.definition: Owning your choices and their outcomes—good or bad—without blaming others, and learning from mistakes.

performance_indicators.text: Build trust in relationships
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply ethics to demonstrate trustworthiness
performance_indicators.definition: Another way to build trust is to demonstrate that you're interested in their well-being -- beyond your own profit potential. Know and appreciate your customers' needs beyond your product. See what you can do to help them meet those needs.

performance_indicators.text: Describe the nature of ethics
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply ethics to demonstrate trustworthiness
performance_indicators.definition: Important to make the general public happy about what happens in the business world (public relations).

performance_indicators.text: Explain reasons for ethical dilemmas
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply ethics to demonstrate trustworthiness
performance_indicators.definition: Ethical dilemmas arise when values, interests, or obligations conflict—such as loyalty versus honesty or profit versus fairness—leaving no clearly right choice.

performance_indicators.text: Recognize and respond to ethical dilemmas
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply ethics to demonstrate trustworthiness
performance_indicators.definition: Identifying situations with competing right-versus-wrong considerations and working through them using values, policies, and sound judgment.

performance_indicators.text: Manage commitments in a timely manner
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply ethics to demonstrate trustworthiness
performance_indicators.definition: Tracking your obligations and deadlines and delivering on them when promised, prioritizing so nothing important is missed.

performance_indicators.text: Develop tolerance for ambiguity
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply ethics to demonstrate trustworthiness
performance_indicators.definition: Staying effective and calm when situations are uncertain or information is incomplete, rather than needing every answer before acting.

performance_indicators.text: Exhibit a positive attitude
performance_indicators.performance_element: Exhibit techniques to manage emotional reactions to people and situations
performance_indicators.definition: Approaching work and people with optimism, energy, and a constructive outlook, which improves morale, relationships, and results.

performance_indicators.text: Demonstrate self-control
performance_indicators.performance_element: Exhibit techniques to manage emotional reactions to people and situations
performance_indicators.definition: Managing your impulses, emotions, and reactions—staying composed under pressure or provocation rather than acting rashly.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the use of feedback for personal growth
performance_indicators.performance_element: Exhibit techniques to manage emotional reactions to people and situations
performance_indicators.definition: Treating constructive criticism as useful information, accepting it without defensiveness, and using it to improve your skills and performance.

performance_indicators.text: Adjust to change
performance_indicators.performance_element: Exhibit techniques to manage emotional reactions to people and situations
performance_indicators.definition: Adapting your attitude and behavior to new circumstances, expectations, or methods with flexibility rather than resistance.

performance_indicators.text: Respect the privacy of others
performance_indicators.performance_element: Identify with others' feelings, needs, and concerns to enhance interpersonal relations
performance_indicators.definition: Honoring people's personal boundaries and confidential information—not prying or sharing what is not yours to share.

performance_indicators.text: Show empathy for others
performance_indicators.performance_element: Identify with others' feelings, needs, and concerns to enhance interpersonal relations
performance_indicators.definition: "I'm sorry we don't have that brand any longer. I know that it's frustrating when you find something you like and then it's no longer available. Can I help you compare what items we do have, so you can find a new choice?"

performance_indicators.text: Maintain the confidentiality of others
performance_indicators.performance_element: Identify with others' feelings, needs, and concerns to enhance interpersonal relations
performance_indicators.definition: Safeguarding sensitive personal or business information entrusted to you and disclosing it only to those authorized to know.

performance_indicators.text: Exhibit cultural sensitivity
performance_indicators.performance_element: Identify with others' feelings, needs, and concerns to enhance interpersonal relations
performance_indicators.definition: A culturally sensitive employee will value diversity, be willing to adapt communication and behavior to be compatible with another's cultural norms, and be willing to learn traditions and characteristics of other cultures. Apply ethics to online communications.

performance_indicators.text: Leverage personality types in business situations
performance_indicators.performance_element: Identify with others' feelings, needs, and concerns to enhance interpersonal relations
performance_indicators.definition: Recognizing different personality styles and adapting your approach to communicate, collaborate, and resolve conflict more effectively with each.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the nature of effective communications
performance_indicators.performance_element: Use communication skills to foster open, honest communications
performance_indicators.definition: Effective communication ensures that messages are not misunderstood, misinterpreted, or lost during the communication process. Carefully choosing the right words, method of communication, and intended audience can eliminate many barriers to communication. Messages should be free of any tone, attitude, and vagueness.

performance_indicators.text: Explain ethical considerations in providing information
performance_indicators.performance_element: Use communication skills to foster open, honest communications
performance_indicators.definition: Ethical business practices ensure that the highest standards of conduct are observed in a company's relationships with everyone in the company or who is affected by its activities. Ethical behavior is lawful and does not harm some while benefiting others.

performance_indicators.text: Foster open, honest communication
performance_indicators.performance_element: Use communication skills to foster open, honest communications
performance_indicators.definition: communication skills influence others' POV - apply problem solving techniques to obtain solutions - manage stressful situations to minimize negative situations - teamwork techniques to accomplish goals. - leadership skills to achieve objectives. - manage internal and external business relationships fostering positive interactions )

performance_indicators.text: Collaborate with others
performance_indicators.performance_element: Use communication skills to foster open, honest communications
performance_indicators.definition: Working cooperatively toward a shared goal—sharing information, dividing tasks, valuing input, and supporting teammates.

performance_indicators.text: Solicit feedback
performance_indicators.performance_element: Use communication skills to foster open, honest communications
performance_indicators.definition: Actively asking others for honest input on your work or behavior so you can identify blind spots and improve.

performance_indicators.text: Use social media to solicit new ideas and solutions
performance_indicators.performance_element: Use communication skills to foster open, honest communications
performance_indicators.definition: Tapping online communities and platforms to crowdsource ideas, gather input, and find solutions from a wide audience.

performance_indicators.text: "Sell" ideas to others
performance_indicators.performance_element: Use communication skills to influence others
performance_indicators.definition: Persuading colleagues or decision-makers to support your proposal by framing its benefits, addressing concerns, and building buy-in.

performance_indicators.text: Persuade others
performance_indicators.performance_element: Use communication skills to influence others
performance_indicators.definition: Influencing people's beliefs or actions through reasoning, evidence, credibility, and appeals to their needs—without manipulation or coercion.

performance_indicators.text: Demonstrate negotiation skills
performance_indicators.performance_element: Use communication skills to influence others
performance_indicators.definition: Working with another party to reach a mutually acceptable agreement through listening, give-and-take, and seeking win-win outcomes.

performance_indicators.text: Use appropriate assertiveness
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage stressful situations to minimize potential negative impact
performance_indicators.definition: Expressing your needs, opinions, and boundaries clearly and confidently while still respecting others—neither passive nor aggressive.

performance_indicators.text: Use conflict-resolution skills
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage stressful situations to minimize potential negative impact
performance_indicators.definition: Addressing disagreements constructively: understanding each side, finding common ground, and reaching a solution that preserves relationships.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the nature of office politics
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage stressful situations to minimize potential negative impact
performance_indicators.definition: Multiple employees battle for dominance over decision-making - can be helpful (clear leader)/harmful (uncontrollable conflict) Use conflict resolution strategies Begin by allowing each party of a dispute to define the problem from his/her point of view. Then, allow each one to suggest a solution to the problem.

performance_indicators.text: Overcome problems and difficulties associated with office politics/turf wars
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage stressful situations to minimize potential negative impact
performance_indicators.definition: Using negotiation, office drama can be resolved in an effective manner. Trying to stay neutral and ethical is the key to solving these types of problems that may arise. It's ok if these issues occur, but not ok if they aren't handled efficiently and effectively.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the nature of stress management
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage stressful situations to minimize potential negative impact
performance_indicators.definition: Recognizing sources of stress and using healthy strategies—prioritizing, time management, breaks, support—to stay productive and well under pressure.

performance_indicators.text: Participate as a team member
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement teamwork techniques to accomplish goals
performance_indicators.definition: Teamwork includes leadership and collaboration. Providing input to a task + facilitating new initiatives. Putting the team in front of yourself is what needs to be done. There's no "I" in team but a "me."

performance_indicators.text: Use consensus-building skills
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement teamwork techniques to accomplish goals
performance_indicators.definition: Guiding a group toward a decision everyone can support by surfacing concerns, finding common ground, and blending viewpoints.

performance_indicators.text: Motivate team members
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement teamwork techniques to accomplish goals
performance_indicators.definition: Inspiring and energizing others to do their best through recognition, shared goals, encouragement, and meeting their needs.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the concept of leadership
performance_indicators.performance_element: Employ leadership skills to achieve workplace objectives
performance_indicators.definition: Leadership is the ability to influence and guide others toward achieving goals through vision, example, communication, and support.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the nature of ethical leadership
performance_indicators.performance_element: Employ leadership skills to achieve workplace objectives
performance_indicators.definition: Leading with integrity—modeling honesty, fairness, and accountability—so that others trust and follow your example.

performance_indicators.text: Model ethical behavior
performance_indicators.performance_element: Employ leadership skills to achieve workplace objectives
performance_indicators.definition: Setting the standard by personally acting ethically, since others take cues from what leaders and coworkers actually do.

performance_indicators.text: Determine personal vision
performance_indicators.performance_element: Employ leadership skills to achieve workplace objectives
performance_indicators.definition: Defining a clear picture of what you want to achieve and become, which gives direction and motivation to your decisions.

performance_indicators.text: Inspire others
performance_indicators.performance_element: Employ leadership skills to achieve workplace objectives
performance_indicators.definition: Energizing and uplifting people through enthusiasm, vision, and example so they feel motivated to contribute their best.

performance_indicators.text: Demonstrate adaptability
performance_indicators.performance_element: Employ leadership skills to achieve workplace objectives
performance_indicators.definition: Adjusting readily to new tasks, roles, and conditions, staying effective when priorities or environments shift.

performance_indicators.text: Develop an achievement orientation
performance_indicators.performance_element: Employ leadership skills to achieve workplace objectives
performance_indicators.definition: Setting high standards and pursuing goals with persistence and a drive to accomplish results.

performance_indicators.text: Challenge the status quo
performance_indicators.performance_element: Employ leadership skills to achieve workplace objectives
performance_indicators.definition: Questioning 'the way things have always been done' and proposing better approaches to improve and innovate.

performance_indicators.text: Lead change
performance_indicators.performance_element: Employ leadership skills to achieve workplace objectives
performance_indicators.definition: Establish a sense of urgency, generate the guiding coalition, and develop a change vision. Journalize business transactions. Journalizing is the process of recording a business transaction in the accounting records. This activity only applies to the double-entry bookkeeping system.

performance_indicators.text: Enlist others in working toward a shared vision
performance_indicators.performance_element: Employ leadership skills to achieve workplace objectives
performance_indicators.definition: Collaboration is a powerful tool and it is important that fellow workers exhibit personality traits such as motivation, positive attitude, empathy, and conflict resolution skills in order to form the perfect work team.

performance_indicators.text: Coach others
performance_indicators.performance_element: Employ leadership skills to achieve workplace objectives
performance_indicators.definition: Make sure they understand what they are supposed to do. Do it sans error. Be patient and always positive when helping others out. Proper coaching can lead to better employee retention and sustainability as a result.

performance_indicators.text: Use power appropriately
performance_indicators.performance_element: Employ leadership skills to achieve workplace objectives
performance_indicators.definition: Exercising authority responsibly and fairly to advance goals, not personal gain, and without intimidating or abusing others.

performance_indicators.text: Recognize/Reward others for their efforts and contributions
performance_indicators.performance_element: Employ leadership skills to achieve workplace objectives
performance_indicators.definition: Acknowledging and appreciating people's work through praise, recognition, or rewards, which boosts morale and motivation.

performance_indicators.text: Treat others with dignity and respect
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage internal and external business relationships to foster positive interactions
performance_indicators.definition: Valuing every person—being courteous, considerate, and fair regardless of role, background, or differences.

performance_indicators.text: Foster positive working relationships
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage internal and external business relationships to foster positive interactions
performance_indicators.definition: To judge: "I have some exciting ideas about the spring promotion. The ideas will involve all of the store's employees. I think you'll like these ideas and I'll be happy to explain the sales contest at the next meeting. This will give you the time you've been needing to research other promotional events." Determine ethical work habits.

performance_indicators.text: Consider conflicting viewpoints
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage internal and external business relationships to foster positive interactions
performance_indicators.definition: Genuinely weighing opinions that differ from your own before deciding, which leads to better, more balanced choices.

performance_indicators.text: Assess long-term value and impact of actions on others
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage internal and external business relationships to foster positive interactions
performance_indicators.definition: Need to realize how own actions might affect others and operations within the company. It's important to take into account all possible outcomes of a decision/action before carrying it out. Usually this will vary depending on the business situation and the position you are in within the company.

performance_indicators.text: Maintain collaborative partnerships with colleagues
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage internal and external business relationships to foster positive interactions
performance_indicators.definition: Building and sustaining cooperative, trusting working relationships that support shared goals over time.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the impact of political relationships within an organization
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage internal and external business relationships to foster positive interactions
performance_indicators.definition: Informal networks of influence and alliances shape how decisions get made; understanding them helps you work effectively and ethically within an organization.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the nature of organizational culture
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage internal and external business relationships to foster positive interactions
performance_indicators.definition: A system of shared assumptions, values, and beliefs, which governs how people behave in organizations. These shared values have a strong influence on the people in the organization and dictate how they dress, act, and perform their jobs.

performance_indicators.text: Interpret and adapt to a business's culture
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage internal and external business relationships to foster positive interactions
performance_indicators.definition: Reading an organization's values, norms, and unwritten rules and adjusting your behavior to fit in and succeed there.

performance_indicators.text: Describe the nature of entrepreneurship
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand fundamental factors about entrepreneurship to recognize its role and importance in the economy
performance_indicators.definition: Entrepreneurship is a dynamic activity which helps the entrepreneur to bring changes in the process of production, innovation in production, new usage of materials, creator of market etc. It is a strong will & attitude to foresee risk and uncertainty and do something new in an effective manner to achieve goals.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the role requirements of entrepreneurs and owners
