performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand fundamental factors about entrepreneurship to recognize its role and importance in the economy
performance_indicators.definition: An entrepreneur is someone who sets up and grows a business; involved in all the operations and activities of the business. Owners are not engaged in day-to-day activities. They are focused on the "big picture" and future direction of the business.

performance_indicators.text: Describe the use of business ethics in entrepreneurship
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand fundamental factors about entrepreneurship to recognize its role and importance in the economy
performance_indicators.definition: Business ethics ensure that a certain basic level of trust exists between consumers and various forms of market participants with businesses. Business ethics are meant to ensure a certain level of trust between consumers and corporations, guaranteeing the public fair and equal treatment.

performance_indicators.text: Describe small-business opportunities in international trade
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand fundamental factors about entrepreneurship to recognize its role and importance in the economy
performance_indicators.definition: Small businesses that export are 20% more productive and have 20% greater job growth than non-exporters, and they are 9% more like to stay financially solvent. Opportunities from global trade also can help fuel the entrepreneurial spirit, open new markets, increase customer base, find new ventures & create new jobs.

performance_indicators.text: Explain forms of financial exchange (cash, credit, debit, electronic funds transfer, etc.)
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand the fundamental principles of money needed to make financial exchanges
performance_indicators.definition: The methods used to transfer value in transactions—cash, check, credit, debit, and electronic funds transfer—each with different convenience, cost, and risk.

performance_indicators.text: Identify types of currency (paper money, coins, banknotes, government bonds, treasury notes, etc.)
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand the fundamental principles of money needed to make financial exchanges
performance_indicators.definition: The forms money takes—paper money (banknotes), coins, and government instruments such as bonds and treasury notes—used as accepted mediums of value.

performance_indicators.text: Describe functions of money (medium of exchange, unit of measure, store of value)
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand the fundamental principles of money needed to make financial exchanges
performance_indicators.definition: Money serves three functions: a medium of exchange (to buy and sell), a unit of measure (to price value), and a store of value (to save for later).

performance_indicators.text: Describe sources of income and compensation
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand the fundamental principles of money needed to make financial exchanges
performance_indicators.definition: Ways people earn money—wages, salary, commission, tips, bonuses, benefits, and investment or business income.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the time value of money
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand the fundamental principles of money needed to make financial exchanges
performance_indicators.definition: The time value of money is based on the premise that receiving a certain amount of money today, rather than the same amount in the future, is more attractive. Money received today is more valuable by the amount of interest the money can earn.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the purposes and importance of credit
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand the fundamental principles of money needed to make financial exchanges
performance_indicators.definition: Credit gives the customer more purchasing power. Credit falls under the category of possession utility. It is a convenience to the customer and in many instances enables a customer to buy a product at a time of need that could not be obtained any other way than with credit.

performance_indicators.text: Explain legal responsibilities associated with consumer financial products and services
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand the fundamental principles of money needed to make financial exchanges
performance_indicators.definition: Providers must follow laws on disclosure, fair lending, and consumer protection; consumers are responsible for understanding terms and meeting their obligations.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the need to save and invest
performance_indicators.performance_element: Analyze financial needs and goals to determine financial requirements
performance_indicators.definition: Saving sets aside money for goals and emergencies; investing grows money over time. Both build financial security and put money to work toward future needs.

performance_indicators.text: Set financial goals
performance_indicators.performance_element: Analyze financial needs and goals to determine financial requirements
performance_indicators.definition: Defining specific, measurable money targets—short- and long-term—that guide saving, spending, and investing decisions.

performance_indicators.text: Develop personal budget
performance_indicators.performance_element: Analyze financial needs and goals to determine financial requirements
performance_indicators.definition: A personal budget is a plan for saving and spending your money based on your income and expenses. Start your budget by defining your needs and your goals, considering both short- and long-term goals. Write them down in a list, with a target date or time frame to reach them.

performance_indicators.text: Determine personal net worth
performance_indicators.performance_element: Analyze financial needs and goals to determine financial requirements
performance_indicators.definition: Net worth is what you own (assets) minus what you owe (liabilities); calculating it shows your overall financial position.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the nature of tax liabilities
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage personal finances to achieve financial goals
performance_indicators.definition: Tax liability is the total amount of tax a person or business legally owes to government based on income, sales, property, or other taxable activity.

performance_indicators.text: Interpret a pay stub
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage personal finances to achieve financial goals
performance_indicators.definition: Your pay stub includes many important things, starting with your name and social security number. It lists the current pay period. Your current and year-to-date earnings, taxes, and deductions are listed.

performance_indicators.text: Write checks
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage personal finances to achieve financial goals
performance_indicators.definition: Completing a check correctly—date, payee, amount in numbers and words, and signature—to authorize payment from a bank account.

performance_indicators.text: Maintain financial records
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage personal finances to achieve financial goals
performance_indicators.definition: Financial records should be done on a regular basis to minimize cost & have more/better control of operations & to get a better understanding of financial information. Day-to-day book-keeping is common for small businesses. Accountants and bookkeepers can help.

performance_indicators.text: Balance a bank account
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage personal finances to achieve financial goals
performance_indicators.definition: Reconciling your records with the bank's statement—accounting for deposits, withdrawals, and fees—to confirm the true available balance.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the nature of charitable giving
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage personal finances to achieve financial goals
performance_indicators.definition: Donating money, goods, or time to nonprofit causes; it supports the community and may offer tax deductions when properly documented.

performance_indicators.text: Manage online accounts
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage personal finances to achieve financial goals
performance_indicators.definition: Safely setting up, monitoring, and maintaining digital financial accounts—tracking activity, protecting passwords, and watching for fraud.

performance_indicators.text: Calculate the cost of credit
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage personal finances to achieve financial goals
performance_indicators.definition: Determining the true cost of borrowing by accounting for the interest rate (APR), fees, and repayment time—not just the amount borrowed.

performance_indicators.text: Demonstrate the wise use of credit
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage personal finances to achieve financial goals
performance_indicators.definition: Borrowing responsibly—only what you can repay, paying on time, and keeping balances low—to build good credit and avoid costly debt.

performance_indicators.text: Validate credit history
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage personal finances to achieve financial goals
performance_indicators.definition: Reviewing your credit report for accuracy, disputing errors, and confirming the record lenders see reflects your true history.

performance_indicators.text: Make responsible financial decisions
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage personal finances to achieve financial goals
performance_indicators.definition: Weighing needs versus wants, costs versus benefits, and short- versus long-term impact to manage money wisely.

performance_indicators.text: Protect against identity theft
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage personal finances to achieve financial goals
performance_indicators.definition: Safeguarding personal and financial information—secure passwords, careful sharing, monitoring accounts—to prevent others from stealing your identity.

performance_indicators.text: Pay bills
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage personal finances to achieve financial goals
performance_indicators.definition: Paying what you owe accurately and on time, through appropriate methods, to avoid late fees and protect your credit.

performance_indicators.text: Apply for a consumer loan
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage personal finances to achieve financial goals
performance_indicators.definition: Requesting borrowed funds from a lender by providing financial information and meeting credit and income requirements, then agreeing to repayment terms.

performance_indicators.text: Control debt
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage personal finances to achieve financial goals
performance_indicators.definition: Managing what you owe so it stays affordable—limiting borrowing, prioritizing high-interest debt, and keeping payments on track.

performance_indicators.text: Prepare personal income tax forms
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage personal finances to achieve financial goals
performance_indicators.definition: Completing required tax returns accurately—reporting income, deductions, and credits—to determine and file what you owe or are refunded.

performance_indicators.text: Discuss options for financing a college education
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage personal finances to achieve financial goals
performance_indicators.definition: Ways to pay for college—savings, scholarships, grants, work-study, and student loans—each with different costs and obligations.

performance_indicators.text: Discuss the nature of retirement planning
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage personal finances to achieve financial goals
performance_indicators.definition: Saving and investing over a career—through accounts such as 401(k)s and IRAs—to build enough income to live on after you stop working.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the nature of estate planning
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage personal finances to achieve financial goals
performance_indicators.definition: Arranging in advance how your assets will be managed and distributed after death—through wills, trusts, and beneficiaries—to carry out your wishes.

performance_indicators.text: Describe types of financial-services providers
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand the use of financial-services providers to aid in financial-goal achievement
performance_indicators.definition: Institutions that offer financial products—banks, credit unions, brokerages, and insurance companies—each serving different needs.

performance_indicators.text: Discuss considerations in selecting a financial-services provider
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand the use of financial-services providers to aid in financial-goal achievement
performance_indicators.definition: Comparing fees, services, convenience, reputation, credentials, and fit with your needs before choosing a provider.

performance_indicators.text: Explain types of investments
performance_indicators.performance_element: Use investment strategies to ensure financial well-being
performance_indicators.definition: Vehicles for growing money—stocks, bonds, mutual funds, and real estate—each with different levels of risk and return.

performance_indicators.text: Describe the concept of insurance
performance_indicators.performance_element: Use risk management products to protect a business's financial well- being
performance_indicators.definition: Insurance provides a company or individual peace of mind and the financial freedom from the devastation of unforeseen occurrences resulting from natural, human, or economic disasters. Some risks may be transferred in the form of insurance.

performance_indicators.text: Determine insurance needs
performance_indicators.performance_element: Identify potential threats and opportunities to protect financial well- being
performance_indicators.definition: Assessing personal or business risks and assets to decide what types and amounts of insurance coverage are appropriate.

performance_indicators.text: Describe the need for financial information
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire a foundational knowledge of accounting to understand its nature and scope
performance_indicators.definition: Accurate financial information lets owners, investors, lenders, and managers judge performance, make decisions, and meet legal requirements.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the concept of accounting
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire a foundational knowledge of accounting to understand its nature and scope
performance_indicators.definition: Accounting is the system of recording, classifying, summarizing, and reporting financial transactions to show a business's financial status.

performance_indicators.text: Discuss the role of ethics in accounting
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire a foundational knowledge of accounting to understand its nature and scope
performance_indicators.definition: Ethics ensures that all members of the company demonstrate integrity and honesty in their work with clients and other professional relationships. Good ethics also prevent accountants from associating themselves with any information that could be misleading or damaging to the client or the organisation.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the use of technology in accounting
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire a foundational knowledge of accounting to understand its nature and scope
performance_indicators.definition: Most businesses use some type of accounting software to record and report their business transactions. Even when using an automated system, you still need to collect and keep your source documents and each business transaction must be separated into its debit and credit parts.

performance_indicators.text: Explain legal considerations for accounting
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire a foundational knowledge of accounting to understand its nature and scope
performance_indicators.definition: The most significant changes to corporate governance and accounting practice came with the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002. The act requires that CEOs, financial officers, accountants, and auditors comply with regulations and procedures designed to ensure accurate representation of companies' financial positions.

performance_indicators.text: Describe the nature of cash flow statements
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement accounting procedures to track money flow and to determine financial status
performance_indicators.definition: A cash flow statement tracks cash coming in and going out over a period—from operations, investing, and financing—showing whether a business can cover its obligations.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the nature of balance sheets
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement accounting procedures to track money flow and to determine financial status
performance_indicators.definition: A balance sheet is a summary of a business' assets, liabilities, and owners' equity. Assets are anything of monetary value that you own and are classified as current or fixed. A current asset is cash or anything that can be converted into cash in a year.

performance_indicators.text: Describe the nature of income statements
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement accounting procedures to track money flow and to determine financial status
performance_indicators.definition: Reports revenues, expenses and profits (or losses) for a period of time. Shows a firm's profit after costs, expenses and taxes. Summarizes all resources that have come into the firm (revenue), all the resources that have left the firm, and the resulting net income or loss.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the role of finance in business
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire a foundational knowledge of finance to understand its nature and scope
performance_indicators.definition: The role of finance in business is to help capital move from investors to businesses. Businesses need finance to assist with operations. This is possible through financial markets and various investment vehicles including loans and short and long term financing.

performance_indicators.text: Discuss the role of ethics in finance
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire a foundational knowledge of finance to understand its nature and scope
performance_indicators.definition: Ethical finance means honest, accurate reporting, avoiding conflicts of interest and fraud, and handling money and information with integrity and transparency.

performance_indicators.text: Explain legal considerations for finance
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire a foundational knowledge of finance to understand its nature and scope
performance_indicators.definition: Finance must comply with laws and regulations on reporting, disclosure, securities, and fraud to protect investors and the public.

performance_indicators.text: Describe the nature of budgets
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage financial resources to ensure solvency
performance_indicators.definition: A budget is an itemized forecast of an individual or company's income and expenses for a designated period of time. This allows the entity to evaluate how to use funds in fixed and discretionary categories as well as provides focus to achieve financial goals and avoid debt. Describe the relationship between accounting and finance.

performance_indicators.text: Discuss the nature of human resources management
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand the role and function of human resources management to obtain a foundational knowledge of its nature and scope
performance_indicators.definition: The main duties involved in human resources management include recruiting, hiring, orientation and training, scheduling, handling employee complaints, assessing performance, discipline, and termination. Recruiting is the process of locating a pool of job applicants and selecting potential employees from this pool.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the role of ethics in human resources management
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand the role and function of human resources management to obtain a foundational knowledge of its nature and scope
performance_indicators.definition: Ethics are standards set by organizations that help ensure that people are treated fairly and honestly. In human resources management this includes making decisions that promote workplace cohesion and establishing standards and procedures that assures in all situations that individuals are treated equally.

performance_indicators.text: Describe the use of technology in human resources management
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand the role and function of human resources management to obtain a foundational knowledge of its nature and scope
performance_indicators.definition: Technology is used more and more in the area of recruiting and hiring employees. Effective sources for locating prospective employees now include Internet career services and company websites. Many companies ask applicants to fill out an application online or at a computer terminal at the company's location.

performance_indicators.text: Orient new employees
performance_indicators.performance_element: Manage staff growth and development to increase productivity and employee satisfaction
performance_indicators.definition: Welcoming and introducing new hires to the company—its policies, culture, people, and their role—so they become productive and comfortable quickly.

performance_indicators.text: Explain marketing and its importance in a global economy
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand marketing's role and function in business to facilitate economic exchanges with customers
performance_indicators.definition: As the global economy has expanded, brands must use technology and communication to establish themselves in a variety of markets. Marketing is a tool that is used to persuade customers and must be employed in order for businesses to sell their products.

performance_indicators.text: Describe marketing functions and related activities
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand marketing's role and function in business to facilitate economic exchanges with customers
performance_indicators.definition: Marketing functions include 4 P's (Price, Promotion, Place, Product). Better 4 P's means more customer loyalty. Explain how businesses can use trade-show/exposition participation to communicate with targeted audiences. A trade show is an exhibit of products of interest to a specific industry.

performance_indicators.text: Explain factors that influence customer/client/business buying behavior
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire foundational knowledge of customer/client/business behavior to understand what motivates decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: Decisions to buy are shaped by needs, motives, perceptions, attitudes, social and cultural influences, price, and the buying situation.

performance_indicators.text: Discuss actions employees can take to achieve the company's desired results
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire foundational knowledge of customer/client/business behavior to understand what motivates decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: Employees advance company goals through good service, quality work, suggestive selling, efficiency, and behavior that reflects the brand.

performance_indicators.text: Describe connections between company actions and results (e.g., influencing consumer buying behavior, gaining market share, etc.)
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire foundational knowledge of customer/client/business behavior to understand what motivates decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: Specific actions—marketing, service, pricing—drive outcomes like influencing buying behavior, gaining market share, and building loyalty; understanding the link guides decisions.

performance_indicators.text: Assess information needs
performance_indicators.performance_element: Use information literacy skills to increase workplace efficiency and effectiveness
performance_indicators.definition: Determining what information is required to make a decision or complete a task before searching, so effort is focused and efficient.

performance_indicators.text: Obtain needed information efficiently
performance_indicators.performance_element: Use information literacy skills to increase workplace efficiency and effectiveness
performance_indicators.definition: Locating accurate information quickly using the right sources and search techniques, without wasting time on irrelevant material.

performance_indicators.text: Evaluate quality and source of information
performance_indicators.performance_element: Use information literacy skills to increase workplace efficiency and effectiveness
performance_indicators.definition: Information is widely accessible in today's market so evaluation tools can be used to determine validity. A reader should determine who the intended audience is, if the data provided is fact or opinion, and if a credible author is attached to the information.

performance_indicators.text: Draw conclusions based on information analysis
performance_indicators.performance_element: Use information literacy skills to increase workplace efficiency and effectiveness
performance_indicators.definition: Examining gathered data, identifying patterns and meaning, and reaching sound, supportable conclusions to guide decisions.

performance_indicators.text: Apply information to accomplish a task
performance_indicators.performance_element: Use information literacy skills to increase workplace efficiency and effectiveness
performance_indicators.definition: Info can be applied to brainstorm promotional strategies based on data, important for marketing (different info gives you different consumer preferences).

performance_indicators.text: Store information for future use
performance_indicators.performance_element: Use information literacy skills to increase workplace efficiency and effectiveness
performance_indicators.definition: Saving and organizing information securely—labeled, backed up, and retrievable—so it can be found and reused when needed.

performance_indicators.text: Discuss the nature of information management
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire a foundational knowledge of information management to understand its nature and scope
performance_indicators.definition: Information management is the process of collecting and analyzing data that can be used in the strategic decision making process for a business. This can support a variety of business functions including developing customer databases, managing electronic purchasing decisions, and monitoring competitor information.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the role of ethics in information management
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire a foundational knowledge of information management to understand its nature and scope
performance_indicators.definition: Handling information honestly and responsibly—respecting privacy, ensuring accuracy, securing data, and using it only for proper purposes.

performance_indicators.text: Explain legal issues associated with information management
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire a foundational knowledge of information management to understand its nature and scope
performance_indicators.definition: Laws govern data privacy, security, ownership, and use; businesses must comply to avoid liability and protect customers and themselves.

performance_indicators.text: Identify ways that technology impacts business
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize information-technology tools to manage and perform work responsibilities
performance_indicators.definition: Technology allows the speed of business transactions to occur globally; financial transactions are becoming instantaneous. Customers have come to expect more choices and higher levels of customer service due to advances in technology.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the role of information systems
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize information-technology tools to manage and perform work responsibilities
performance_indicators.definition: An information system is a set of procedures and methods that generate, store, analyze, and distribute information for use in making business decisions. Such systems organize, collect, and store data for current use or future decisions. Most information systems rely on data about current customers, sales reports, and inventory levels.

performance_indicators.text: Discuss principles of computer systems
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize information-technology tools to manage and perform work responsibilities
performance_indicators.definition: A system of interconnected computers that share a central storage system and various peripheral devices such as printers, scanners, or routers. Each computer connected to the system can operate independently, but has the ability to communicate with other external devices and computers.

performance_indicators.text: Use basic operating systems
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize information-technology tools to manage and perform work responsibilities
performance_indicators.definition: Operating a computer's core software to manage files, run programs, and control settings and devices.

performance_indicators.text: Describe the scope of the internet
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize information-technology tools to manage and perform work responsibilities
performance_indicators.definition: The internet is the worldwide, publicly accessible system of interconnected computer networks that transmit data by packet switching using the standard IP.

performance_indicators.text: Demonstrate basic email functions
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize information-technology tools to manage and perform work responsibilities
performance_indicators.definition: Sending, receiving, replying, forwarding, attaching files, and organizing messages in an email program.

performance_indicators.text: Demonstrate personal information management/productivity applications
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize information-technology tools to manage and perform work responsibilities
performance_indicators.definition: Using tools like calendars, contacts, tasks, and notes to organize schedules, information, and work.

performance_indicators.text: Demonstrate basic web-search skills
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize information-technology tools to manage and perform work responsibilities
performance_indicators.definition: Finding relevant, credible information online using search engines, keywords, and filters effectively.

performance_indicators.text: Demonstrate basic word processing skills
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize information-technology tools to manage and perform work responsibilities
performance_indicators.definition: Creating, formatting, editing, and saving documents in a word-processing program.

performance_indicators.text: Demonstrate basic presentation applications
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize information-technology tools to manage and perform work responsibilities
performance_indicators.definition: Building and delivering slide presentations with text, images, and visuals using presentation software.

performance_indicators.text: Demonstrate basic database applications
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize information-technology tools to manage and perform work responsibilities
performance_indicators.definition: Maintaining a database for each customer group enables the company to better promote information to each individual based on their product needs and wants. Zip code information saves money on mailings due to bulk rates.

performance_indicators.text: Demonstrate basic spreadsheet applications
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize information-technology tools to manage and perform work responsibilities
performance_indicators.definition: Entering, organizing, and calculating data in spreadsheets using formulas, functions, and charts.

performance_indicators.text: Use an integrated business software application package
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize information-technology tools to manage and perform work responsibilities
performance_indicators.definition: Working across a suite of connected programs (word processing, spreadsheet, presentation, database) that share data to perform business tasks.

performance_indicators.text: Demonstrate collaborative/groupware applications
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize information-technology tools to manage and perform work responsibilities
performance_indicators.definition: Using shared online tools that let teams work together on documents, projects, and communication in real time.

performance_indicators.text: Create and post basic web page
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize information-technology tools to manage and perform work responsibilities
performance_indicators.definition: Building a simple web page with text, images, and links and publishing it online.

performance_indicators.text: Collaborate on and aggregate complex internal documents to create a common voice
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize information-technology tools to manage and perform work responsibilities
performance_indicators.definition: Combining input from multiple contributors into a unified document with consistent style, tone, and message.

performance_indicators.text: Describe the nature of business records
performance_indicators.performance_element: Maintain business records to facilitate business operations
performance_indicators.definition: meeting minutes, memoranda, employment contracts, and accounting source document, revenue/income/capita statements, etc. To be reviewed for monitoring process, accidents, patterns, employee evaluation

performance_indicators.text: Maintain customer records
performance_indicators.performance_element: Maintain business records to facilitate business operations
performance_indicators.definition: Keeping customer information accurate, current, complete, and secure so it can support service and decision-making.

performance_indicators.text: Describe current business trends
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire information to guide business decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: Assessing current business trends is important to both business and professional growth. Business owners and entrepreneurs should keep up-to-date through a variety of means. Classes are offered at vocational/technical schools, two- and four-year colleges, and online.

performance_indicators.text: Monitor internal records for business information
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire information to guide business decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: Regularly reviewing a company's own records—sales, inventory, finances—to spot trends and inform decisions.

performance_indicators.text: Conduct an environmental scan to obtain business information
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire information to guide business decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: Systematically gathering information about external conditions—market, competitors, trends, regulations—to guide strategy.

performance_indicators.text: Interpret statistical findings
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire information to guide business decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: Reading and making sense of statistical data—averages, percentages, trends—to draw accurate, useful conclusions.

performance_indicators.text: Translate research findings into actionable business recommendations
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire information to guide business decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: Turning data and analysis into clear, practical recommendations that managers can act on.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the principles of data analysis
performance_indicators.performance_element: Create and access databases to acquire information for business decision- making
performance_indicators.definition: Data analysis is examining raw data to find patterns and meaning—collecting, cleaning, processing, and interpreting it to support decisions.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the nature of tools that can be used to access information in the database system
performance_indicators.performance_element: Create and access databases to acquire information for business decision- making
performance_indicators.definition: Queries, reports, and software tools let users retrieve and view specific information stored in a database.

performance_indicators.text: Access information in the database system
performance_indicators.performance_element: Create and access databases to acquire information for business decision- making
performance_indicators.definition: Retrieving specific records or data from a database using queries, searches, or reports.

performance_indicators.text: Discuss the nature of data mining
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply data mining methods to acquire pertinent information for business decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: Data mining analyzes large data sets to discover hidden patterns, relationships, and trends useful for business decisions.

performance_indicators.text: Describe data mining tools and techniques
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply data mining methods to acquire pertinent information for business decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: Methods and software—classification, clustering, association, and predictive modeling—used to extract insights from large data sets.

performance_indicators.text: Discuss the importance of ethics in data mining
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply data mining methods to acquire pertinent information for business decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: Mining data ethically means respecting privacy, obtaining proper consent, securing data, and avoiding misuse or discrimination.

performance_indicators.text: Demonstrate basic data mining techniques
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply data mining methods to acquire pertinent information for business decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: Applying simple methods to explore a data set and surface patterns or relationships.

performance_indicators.text: Interpret data mining findings
performance_indicators.performance_element: Apply data mining methods to acquire pertinent information for business decision-making
performance_indicators.definition: Making sense of patterns discovered through data mining and judging what they mean for the business.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the nature of operations
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand operation's role and function in business to value its contribution to a company
performance_indicators.definition: Operations include the activities involved in the daily functions of the business to generate revenue.

performance_indicators.text: Discuss the role of ethics in operations
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand operation's role and function in business to value its contribution to a company
performance_indicators.definition: Business operations employees should not disclose information unless authorized and decisions should not be made unless it is permitted.

performance_indicators.text: Describe the use of technology in operations
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand operation's role and function in business to value its contribution to a company
performance_indicators.definition: Technology—software, automation, tracking systems—improves operations by increasing efficiency, accuracy, and visibility across processes.

performance_indicators.text: Describe health and safety regulations in business
performance_indicators.performance_element: Adhere to health and safety regulations to support a safe work environment
performance_indicators.definition: Businesses must adhere to health and safety regulations established by the U.S.Department of Labor. Workplace safety regulations are enforced by the Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) through inspections and fines. Employers must maintain health and safety records and provide safety training.

performance_indicators.text: Report noncompliance with business health and safety regulations
performance_indicators.performance_element: Adhere to health and safety regulations to support a safe work environment
performance_indicators.definition: Promptly informing the appropriate person or authority when safety or health rules are being violated, to protect people and the business.

performance_indicators.text: Follow instructions for use of equipment, tools, and machinery
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement safety procedures to minimize loss
performance_indicators.definition: Choose learning methods Any method of learning should support individuals, teams or the organization as a whole to build capability that meets business needs.

performance_indicators.text: Follow safety precautions
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement safety procedures to minimize loss
performance_indicators.definition: Observing established safety rules and practices to prevent injuries, accidents, and loss on the job.

performance_indicators.text: Maintain a safe work environment
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement safety procedures to minimize loss
performance_indicators.definition: Creating a safe work environment is important due to the significant financial impact these policies can have on a business.

performance_indicators.text: Explain procedures for handling accidents
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement safety procedures to minimize loss
performance_indicators.definition: Immediately make sure if everyone is alright, call 911 ASAP, report and record the incident, take steps to make sure the incident never happens again. Explain how frequency schedules are determined for guestroom cleaning Frequency schedules indicate how often items on inventory lists are to be cleaned or maintained.

performance_indicators.text: Handle and report emergency situations
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement safety procedures to minimize loss
performance_indicators.definition: Responding to emergencies calmly using proper procedures and notifying the right people to keep people safe and limit damage.

performance_indicators.text: Explain routine security precautions
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement security policies/procedures to minimize chance for loss
performance_indicators.definition: Encourage and assist employees to be prepared for personal, natural, and technological emergencies. Review and update the call down list for emergency response teams. Conduct training for employees on physical security precautions. Develop emergency response and business resumption plans around these emergencies.

performance_indicators.text: Follow established security procedures/policies
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement security policies/procedures to minimize chance for loss
performance_indicators.definition: Adhering to the company's rules for protecting people, property, and information from theft, harm, or loss.

performance_indicators.text: Protect company information and intangibles
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement security policies/procedures to minimize chance for loss
performance_indicators.definition: Safeguarding sensitive assets—data, trade secrets, intellectual property, and reputation—from theft, leaks, or misuse.

performance_indicators.text: Explain information privacy, security, and confidentiality considerations in business
performance_indicators.performance_element: Comply with security rules, regulations, and codes (e.g., property, privacy, access, confidentiality) to protect customer and company information, reputation, and image
performance_indicators.definition: Businesses must protect personal and proprietary information from unauthorized access and use, balancing access with privacy and legal duties.

performance_indicators.text: Maintain data security
performance_indicators.performance_element: Comply with security rules, regulations, and codes (e.g., property, privacy, access, confidentiality) to protect customer and company information, reputation, and image
performance_indicators.definition: Protecting digital information through measures like passwords, encryption, backups, and access controls to prevent breaches and loss.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the nature of project management
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire a foundational knowledge of project management to understand its nature and scope
performance_indicators.definition: Project management is using knowledge, skills, and techniques to execute a temporary group activity designed to develop a product, service, or outcome both effectively and efficiently. There are five processes needed in project management: initiating, planning, executing, monitoring, and controlling and closing.

performance_indicators.text: Describe the role of ethics in project management
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire a foundational knowledge of project management to understand its nature and scope
performance_indicators.definition: Ethical project management means honest reporting of progress and costs, fair treatment of stakeholders, and responsible use of resources.

performance_indicators.text: Plan project
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize project management skills to improve workflow and minimize costs
performance_indicators.definition: Defining a project's goals, tasks, timeline, resources, and responsibilities before work begins.

performance_indicators.text: Monitor projects and take corrective actions
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize project management skills to improve workflow and minimize costs
performance_indicators.definition: Once you've identified areas where need improvement,you will have to take corrective actions to get your project back on track. An example is refocusing the scope of your project. Project failure, risk tracking, and stakeholder management are not types of corrective actions to take if your project has derailed.

performance_indicators.text: Evaluate project success
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize project management skills to improve workflow and minimize costs
performance_indicators.definition: Judging whether a project met its goals, budget, timeline, and quality standards, and what can be learned for next time.

performance_indicators.text: Identify resources needed for project
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize project management skills to improve workflow and minimize costs
performance_indicators.definition: Determining the people, money, materials, time, and tools a project requires to be completed.

performance_indicators.text: Develop project plan
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize project management skills to improve workflow and minimize costs
performance_indicators.definition: Creating a detailed roadmap of a project's scope, schedule, resources, tasks, and milestones to guide execution.

performance_indicators.text: Apply project-management tools to monitor and communicate project progress
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize project management skills to improve workflow and minimize costs
performance_indicators.definition: Using tools such as Gantt charts, dashboards, and software to track tasks and keep stakeholders informed of status.

performance_indicators.text: Evaluate project results
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize project management skills to improve workflow and minimize costs
performance_indicators.definition: Assessing a completed project's outcomes against its objectives to measure success and capture lessons learned.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the nature and scope of purchasing
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement purchasing activities to obtain business supplies, equipment, resources, and services
performance_indicators.definition: The actions that a company takes to bring in their products Businesses want to purchase the highest quality goods at the lowest possible cost The actions that a company takes to bring in their products Explain buying strategies Attending industry trade shows offers a method of seeing new products and determining what will complement the business' product line.

performance_indicators.text: Place orders/reorders
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement purchasing activities to obtain business supplies, equipment, resources, and services
performance_indicators.definition: Purchasing needed goods or supplies—initial orders and restocking—at the right quantity and time to keep operations running.

performance_indicators.text: Maintain inventory of supplies
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement purchasing activities to obtain business supplies, equipment, resources, and services
performance_indicators.definition: Tracking stock levels and reordering as needed so the right supplies are available without overstocking.

performance_indicators.text: Discuss the importance of utilizing ethical purchasing methods
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement purchasing activities to obtain business supplies, equipment, resources, and services
performance_indicators.definition: Buying honestly and fairly—avoiding kickbacks, conflicts of interest, and favoritism—and treating suppliers equitably.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the impact of the purchasing process on productivity
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement purchasing activities to obtain business supplies, equipment, resources, and services
performance_indicators.definition: Efficient, well-timed purchasing keeps materials available and costs controlled, directly affecting how smoothly and profitably work flows.

performance_indicators.text: Manage the bid process in purchasing
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement purchasing activities to obtain business supplies, equipment, resources, and services
performance_indicators.definition: Soliciting and comparing competitive bids from suppliers to obtain the best value fairly and transparently.

performance_indicators.text: Select vendors
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement purchasing activities to obtain business supplies, equipment, resources, and services
performance_indicators.definition: Choosing suppliers based on price, quality, reliability, service, and terms to meet the business's needs.

performance_indicators.text: Evaluate vendor performance
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement purchasing activities to obtain business supplies, equipment, resources, and services
performance_indicators.definition: Measuring how well suppliers deliver on quality, timeliness, price, and service to decide whether to continue with them.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the concept of production
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand production's role and function in business to recognize its need in an organization
performance_indicators.definition: Production decisions concentrate on what goods to produce, how to produce them, the costs of producing them, and optimizing the mix of resource inputs used in their production This production information can then be combined with market information (like demand and marginal revenue) to determine the quantity of products to produce and the optimum pricing Explain the concept of

performance_indicators.text: Identify quality-control measures
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement quality-control processes to minimize errors and to expedite workflow
performance_indicators.definition: Quality control is a process through which a business seeks to ensure that product quality is maintained or improved and manufacturing errors are reduced or eliminated. Quality control requires the business to create an environment in which both management and employees strive for perfection.

performance_indicators.text: Utilize quality control methods at work
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement quality-control processes to minimize errors and to expedite workflow
performance_indicators.definition: Applying inspection and process checks to catch and prevent defects, keeping products and services up to standard.

performance_indicators.text: Describe crucial elements of a quality culture
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement quality-control processes to minimize errors and to expedite workflow
performance_indicators.definition: A quality culture is an organization-wide commitment to high standards—continuous improvement, accountability, and customer focus at every level.

performance_indicators.text: Resolve problems with suppliers' quality issues
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement quality-control processes to minimize errors and to expedite workflow
performance_indicators.definition: Identifying defects or shortfalls in supplier goods and working with the supplier to correct them and prevent recurrence.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the nature of overhead/operating costs
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement expense-control strategies to enhance a business's financial wellbeing
performance_indicators.definition: Operating costs are the recurring expenses, which are related to the operation of a business, piece of equipment or facility For a commercial enterprise, operating costs fall into two broad categories: Overhead/fixed costs: costs that are the same whether the operation is closed or running at 100% capacity (examples: rent, management salaries, etc.) Variable costs: costs that

performance_indicators.text: Explain employee's role in expense control
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement expense-control strategies to enhance a business's financial wellbeing
performance_indicators.definition: Employees control expenses by reducing waste, using resources wisely, and following cost-conscious practices in daily work.

performance_indicators.text: Organize and prioritize work
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement organizational skills to improve efficiency and work flow
performance_indicators.definition: Arranging tasks by importance and deadline and managing time so the most critical work gets done efficiently.

performance_indicators.text: Coordinate work with that of team members
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement organizational skills to improve efficiency and work flow
performance_indicators.definition: Aligning your tasks and timing with teammates' so work flows smoothly and goals are met together.

performance_indicators.text: Coordinate activities with those of other departments
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement organizational skills to improve efficiency and work flow
performance_indicators.definition: Aligning work across departments so efforts complement rather than conflict, supporting overall company goals.

performance_indicators.text: Monitor and ensure completion of delegated tasks
performance_indicators.performance_element: Maintain work flow to enhance productivity
performance_indicators.definition: Following up on work assigned to others to confirm it is done correctly and on time.

performance_indicators.text: Streamline work processes
performance_indicators.performance_element: Maintain work flow to enhance productivity
performance_indicators.definition: Improving how work is done by removing unnecessary steps, reducing waste, and increasing efficiency.

performance_indicators.text: Comply with policies and procedures for use of property and equipment
performance_indicators.performance_element: Maintain property and equipment to facilitate ongoing business activities
performance_indicators.definition: Using company property and equipment according to established rules to keep it safe, functional, and well-maintained.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the concept of supply chain
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand supply chain management's role in business to appreciate its contributions to efficiency and profitability
performance_indicators.definition: A supply chain is the network of organizations and steps that move a product from raw materials through production to the final customer.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the benefits of supply chain collaboration
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand supply chain management's role in business to appreciate its contributions to efficiency and profitability
performance_indicators.definition: Partners sharing information and coordinating across the supply chain reduce costs, delays, and waste while improving service and responsiveness.

performance_indicators.text: Maintain appropriate personal appearance
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire self-development skills to enhance relationships and improve efficiency in the work environment
performance_indicators.definition: Maintaining your personal appearance is necessary as employees represent the business in which he/she is employed. Personal appearance includes facial expressions, body language, as well as clothes and grooming.

performance_indicators.text: Demonstrate systematic behavior
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire self-development skills to enhance relationships and improve efficiency in the work environment
performance_indicators.definition: Demonstrating systematic behavior is immensely profitable in the workplace.

performance_indicators.text: Set personal goals
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire self-development skills to enhance relationships and improve efficiency in the work environment
performance_indicators.definition: Defining specific, achievable objectives for yourself, with steps and timelines, to direct effort and measure progress.

performance_indicators.text: Balance personal and professional responsibilities
performance_indicators.performance_element: Acquire self-development skills to enhance relationships and improve efficiency in the work environment
performance_indicators.definition: Managing work and personal life so both receive appropriate time and attention, sustaining well-being and performance.

performance_indicators.text: Adhere to company protocols and policies
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand and follow company rules and regulations to maintain employment
performance_indicators.definition: Following the organization's established rules, procedures, and standards in your work.

performance_indicators.text: Follow rules of conduct
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand and follow company rules and regulations to maintain employment
performance_indicators.definition: Behaving according to the workplace's expected standards of professional and ethical conduct.

performance_indicators.text: Follow chain of command
performance_indicators.performance_element: Understand and follow company rules and regulations to maintain employment
performance_indicators.definition: Respecting the organization's lines of authority—reporting to and communicating through the proper levels.

performance_indicators.text: Determine the nature of organizational goals
performance_indicators.performance_element: Achieve organizational goals to contribute to company growth
performance_indicators.definition: Understanding what an organization is trying to achieve—its mission, objectives, and priorities—so work aligns with them.

performance_indicators.text: Ascertain employee's role in meeting organizational goals
performance_indicators.performance_element: Achieve organizational goals to contribute to company growth
performance_indicators.definition: Recognizing how your specific tasks and performance contribute to the company's larger objectives.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the need for innovation skills
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize critical-thinking skills to determine best options/outcomes
performance_indicators.definition: Innovation is necessary for an organization to continue to move forward in the marketplace and meet customer needs.

performance_indicators.text: Make decisions
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize critical-thinking skills to determine best options/outcomes
performance_indicators.definition: Choosing among options by weighing information, alternatives, and consequences to reach the best outcome.

performance_indicators.text: Demonstrate problem-solving skills
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize critical-thinking skills to determine best options/outcomes
performance_indicators.definition: Problem solving skills include decision-making, reasoning (logic), and creativity.

performance_indicators.text: Demonstrate appropriate creativity
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize critical-thinking skills to determine best options/outcomes
performance_indicators.definition: Make connections, Ask questions, Make observations, Networking, Experimenting.

performance_indicators.text: Use time-management skills
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize critical-thinking skills to determine best options/outcomes
performance_indicators.definition: Stay organized, prioritize tasks, set short-term & long-term goals, communicate, plan delegate, and manage stress.

performance_indicators.text: Assess personal interests and skills needed for success in business
performance_indicators.performance_element: Participate in career planning to enhance job-success potential
performance_indicators.definition: communication skills, technology skills, leadership, creative and innovative skills.

performance_indicators.text: Analyze employer expectations in the business environment
performance_indicators.performance_element: Participate in career planning to enhance job-success potential
performance_indicators.definition: Employers expect a lot of things from their employees, including cooperation, initiative, and a strong work ethic. Employees need to display a willingness to follow directions, to learn job tasks and skills, and to take on responsibility on the job. Employers expect you to manage yourself, your time, and your emotions in the workplace.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the rights of workers
performance_indicators.performance_element: Participate in career planning to enhance job-success potential
performance_indicators.definition: Employees are legally entitled to protections such as fair pay, safe conditions, freedom from discrimination, and the right to organize.

performance_indicators.text: Identify sources of career information
performance_indicators.performance_element: Participate in career planning to enhance job-success potential
performance_indicators.definition: Knowing where to find career data—labor websites, professionals, schools, associations—to make informed career choices.

performance_indicators.text: Identify tentative occupational interest
performance_indicators.performance_element: Participate in career planning to enhance job-success potential
performance_indicators.definition: Exploring and naming careers that fit your interests, skills, and values as a starting point for planning.

performance_indicators.text: Explain employment opportunities in business
performance_indicators.performance_element: Participate in career planning to enhance job-success potential
performance_indicators.definition: The range of jobs and career paths available across business functions such as marketing, finance, management, and operations.

performance_indicators.text: Explain career opportunities in entrepreneurship
performance_indicators.performance_element: Participate in career planning to enhance job-success potential
performance_indicators.definition: Paths in starting and running your own venture, including the roles, rewards, and risks of being a business owner.

performance_indicators.text: Utilize job-search strategies
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement job-seeking skills to obtain employment
performance_indicators.definition: Using methods—job boards, networking, applications, referrals—to find and pursue employment opportunities effectively.

performance_indicators.text: Complete a job application
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement job-seeking skills to obtain employment
performance_indicators.definition: Filling out an employer's application accurately, completely, and professionally to be considered for a job.

performance_indicators.text: Interview for a job
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement job-seeking skills to obtain employment
performance_indicators.definition: Presenting yourself effectively to an employer—prepared, professional, and articulate—to demonstrate fit for a position.

performance_indicators.text: Write a follow-up letter after job interviews
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement job-seeking skills to obtain employment
performance_indicators.definition: Sending a prompt, courteous note thanking the interviewer, reaffirming interest, and reinforcing your qualifications.

performance_indicators.text: Write a cover letter
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement job-seeking skills to obtain employment
performance_indicators.definition: Composing a letter that accompanies a resume, introducing yourself and highlighting why you fit the specific job.

performance_indicators.text: Prepare a resume
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement job-seeking skills to obtain employment
performance_indicators.definition: Creating a clear, organized summary of your skills, experience, and education to present to employers.

performance_indicators.text: Use networking techniques to identify employment opportunities
performance_indicators.performance_element: Implement job-seeking skills to obtain employment
performance_indicators.definition: Building and using professional relationships to learn about and access job openings, often before they are advertised.

performance_indicators.text: Describe techniques for obtaining work experience (e.g., volunteer activities, internships)
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize career-advancement activities to enhance professional development
performance_indicators.definition: Analyze the impact of technology on marketing - Customer relationship management (CRM) involves finding customers & keeping them satisfied through: database marketing, creating and maintaining customer lists, and online customer surveys. - Television advertising has less impact than it used to.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the need for ongoing education as a worker
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize career-advancement activities to enhance professional development
performance_indicators.definition: changes in laws and regulations become more competent in job/field learn new techniques improve employability skills advance in career professional development can continue through academics (degree, graduate or master programs) Social Networking provides an opportunity to connect and share ideas with other professionals (twitter, facebook, linkedin, etc).

performance_indicators.text: Explain possible advancement patterns for jobs
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize career-advancement activities to enhance professional development
performance_indicators.definition: In many industries, jobs can be categorized based on skill level. One can advance from one level to the next through experience, education, attrition, and promotion. Entry-level jobs usually require no prior experience and involve only limited decision making. Career-sustaining jobs are the next level, requiring more skill and experience.

performance_indicators.text: Identify skills needed to enhance career progression
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize career-advancement activities to enhance professional development
performance_indicators.definition: Consideration should be given to employees who seek to better themselves Good employees should display personal drive and motivation combined with passion. Also, negotiation and communication skills help.

performance_indicators.text: Utilize resources that can contribute to professional development (e.g., trade journals/periodicals, professional/trade associations, classes/seminars, trade shows, and mentors)
performance_indicators.performance_element: Utilize career-advancement activities to enhance professional development
performance_indicators.definition: Publications such as e-mail newsletters, on-line discussion boards and industry magazines can provide info on the latest technology and trends in the industry.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the concept of management
performance_indicators.performance_element: Recognize management's role to understand its contribution to business success
performance_indicators.definition: Management is the coordination of activities a business uses to achieve results; this includes both marketing and innovations. Describe the nature of managerial control.

performance_indicators.text: Explain factors that affect management
performance_indicators.performance_element: Recognize management's role to understand its contribution to business success
performance_indicators.definition: Internal and external factors—resources, employees, the economy, competition, technology, and regulations—shape how a business must be managed.

performance_indicators.text: Explain the nature of risk management
performance_indicators.performance_element: Identify potential business threats and opportunities to protect a business's financial well-being
performance_indicators.definition: Marketing is the management of the exchanges between an organization and its stakeholders -- customers, employees, community groups, and others. The two primary roles of marketing are to attain and retain target customers. Markets can be defined from both supply and demand perspectives.

performance_indicators.text: Conduct a risk assessment of an event
performance_indicators.performance_element: Identify potential business threats and opportunities to protect a business's financial well-being
performance_indicators.definition: Identifying what could go wrong with an event, judging the likelihood and impact of each risk, and planning to prevent or reduce them.
